'''Rotas V''' was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that occupied the fifth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="CSWE" /> in the [[Rotas system/Legends|Rotas system]], a part of the [[Yminis sector/Legends|Yminis sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to a group of [[Rotas V indigenous fighters|indigenous fighters]] that was affiliated with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="Insider84">{{InsiderCite|84|Guide to the Grand Army of the Republic}}</ref>

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Republic leadership was reluctant to allow widespread use of the [[Kama/Legends|''kama'']], a [[Commanding officer/Legends|command]] [[Clothing/Legends|skirt]] and a potentially controversial symbol traditionally associated with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior culture, in the ranks of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. That changed when it was pointed out that the indigenous [[Soldier/Legends|fighters]] of Rotas V also wore ''kamas'',<ref name="Insider84" /> and the skirts subsequently worn by the Republic's [[Clone Commander/Legends|clone trooper commanders]] were modeled after those of the Rotas V fighters.<ref name="CVD">[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary|''Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary'', 2006 edition]]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Rotas V was first mentioned in the [[2005]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]'', which was authored by [[James Luceno]].<ref name="Ep3TVD">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Rotas [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Rotas V, in grid square S-14.<ref name="TEA" />
